name: pytest-xdist-session-fixture-runs-per-worker
tags: pytest-xdist, session fixture, autouse, per-worker, flaky test, test_logs.db, parallel contention
body:
A pytest fixture with `scope="session", autouse=True` does NOT run once per overall test invocation under pytest-xdist's `-n auto` — it runs once PER WORKER PROCESS, since each xdist worker is effectively its own independent pytest session. Code that assumes "session scope = exactly once" (e.g. a merge-in-memory-DB-to-file step at session teardown) will misbehave under xdist: multiple workers independently attempt the same one-time action, which either races or (if the action has a latent bug) makes that bug look like "always fails" rather than surfacing as an obvious intermittent race.

**Why:** `tests/test_logs.db` (claude-hooks-dev) was 0 bytes for weeks — `_merge_mem_to_file` had a real schema-init bug (assumed the target file already had a `hook_logs` table, only ever ran `ALTER TABLE` on it), masked by a bare `except Exception: pass`. Also explains why `test_parallel_faster_than_sequential` (a timing-sensitive perf assertion) only flaked when run under `-n auto`, never in isolation or without xdist — worker resource contention, not the code under test.

**How to apply:** When a "session-scoped, runs once" test fixture behaves unexpectedly under a parallel test runner (xdist, pytest-parallel, etc.), check whether it's actually running multiple times (once per worker) before debugging the fixture's own logic. More generally: never swallow exceptions bare (`except Exception: pass`) in test fixture teardown — print or log them, even in a "best effort, don't fail the test run" cleanup step, or a real bug can hide indefinitely.
